summary: Stunning soundboard capture with crystal‑clear mix and a fire‑filled show highlighted by a soaring Dark Star, Morning Dew and Lovelight.
review: The recording is an exceptionally clean soundboard mix that sounds like it was mastered for release – crisp drums, punchy bass and front‑center vocals with only the faintest tape hiss. Musically the night is a jam‑fest: the first set burns through Morning Dew, Cryptical Envelopment > The Other One and Alligator, while the second set never lets up, delivering one of ’69’s finest Dark Star, a spine‑tingling St Stephen and an enormous Lovelight. Reviewers rave about the telepathic interplay and call it one of the top three 1969 performances.

ai_rating.rationale: All reviews (except a filler comment) are 4‑5 stars, praising both the performance and the mix; the show is described as exemplary and the recording as “modern” and “excellent”.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, balanced mix with hard‑left/right drums, punchy bass and clear vocals. Slight tape hiss noted but negligible; described as sounding like a release‑ready master.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set blends early‑68 raw tones with 73‑era elegance, building into a powerful jam sequence; second set maintains nonstop momentum, delivering high‑energy peaks and seamless transitions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar tone is described as “extra twang” and “ripping down the walls”; vocals are front‑and‑center and in key.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for “nice chops”, big telepathic chords, and leading the Lovelight peak; his improvisational cues are highlighted throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Provides slick bass lines that add depth, though a reviewer wished it was turned up more.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mix places drums hard left/right with strong presence, contributing to the modern‑sound feel.
